tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi there! I'm new to using this kind of tool. I work in community arts and social development, and I wanted to see if there's any information already stored here about arts programs and the social impact work we've been doing. Can you help me look for that?
I'd be happy to help you look for any stored information about your arts programs and social impact work! Let me search through the memory storage to see what's already there.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "arts programs community arts social development impact work"}

messages.content:
The search came back empty — there isn't any information stored yet about your arts programs or social impact work.

This memory system is currently a blank slate for you. Here's what it can do:

- **Store** information about your programs, projects, or approaches that you want to remember for future reference
- **Search** through stored memories later to quickly pull up past details
- **Organize** notes about your work, contacts, methods, or anything else you find useful

Would you like to add some information about your community arts and social development work? I can help you store details about your programs, goals, or approaches — whatever would be most helpful for you to have readily available later.

Yes, absolutely! I've been working on some really exciting projects lately that I'd love to store here for future reference.

Right now I'm leading a community arts initiative that partners with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods. We bring in teaching artists to run after-school programs focused on visual arts, theater, and music—it's all about using creative expression to help young people build confidence and connect with their community.

I'm also part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in our district. We recently did a survey showing that 78% of parents believe arts programs improve their kids' academic performance, but the budget keeps getting cut.

Oh, and I personally prefer collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones—I think the social connection that happens *during* creation matters just as much as the final product. That's something I always try to emphasize in my advocacy work.

Would you like me to tell you more about any of these projects, or should I just share everything and you can help me organize it?
That's wonderful work you're doing! What you've shared already has great detail — let me store it for you now, and you can always add more later as things evolve.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User works in community arts and social development. They lead: (1) A community arts initiative partnering with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, bringing in teaching artists for after-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music — focused on helping young people build confidence and connect with their community. (2) Part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in their district. Recently conducted a survey showing 78% of parents believe arts programs improve kids' academic performance, yet budget cuts continue. Personal philosophy: prefers collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones — believes social connection during creation matters as much as the final product. This belief influences their advocacy work."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User works in community arts and social development. They lead: (1) A community arts initiative partnering with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, bringing in teaching artists for after-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music — focused on helping young people build confidence and connect with their community. (2) Part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in their district. Recently conducted a survey showing 78% of parents believe arts programs improve kids' academic performance, yet budget cuts continue. Personal philosophy: prefers collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones — believes social connection during creation matters as much as the final product. This belief influences their advocacy work."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've stored all of that information under memory ID **m2**. Here's what now lives in your personal memory:

- **Community Arts Initiative** — After-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music at schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, focused on youth confidence-building and community connection
- **Advocacy Coalition Work** — Pushing for public arts education funding; you have survey data showing 78% of parents link arts to academic performance
- **Your Philosophy** — Preference for collaborative, process-oriented art forms; you believe the social connection during creation is just as valuable as the final product

This will be here whenever you need to reference it — for future conversations, grant applications, or just to keep the details handy. 

Would you like to add anything else about your work, or is there anything else I can help you with?
Thanks for storing all that! Now I'm curious—what all do I have saved in here? Can you show me everything that's currently stored so I can see the full picture of what context is available?

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "User works in community arts and social development. They lead: (1) A community arts initiative partnering with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, bringing in teaching artists for after-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music — focused on helping young people build confidence and connect with their community. (2) Part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in their district. Recently conducted a survey showing 78% of parents believe arts programs improve kids' academic performance, yet budget cuts continue. Personal philosophy: prefers collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones — believes social connection during creation matters as much as the final product. This belief influences their advocacy work."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's everything currently stored in your memory:

**Memory ID: m2**
> User works in community arts and social development. They lead: (1) A community arts initiative partnering with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, bringing in teaching artists for after-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music — focused on helping young people build confidence and connect with their community. (2) Part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in their district. Recently conducted a survey showing 78% of parents believe arts programs improve kids' academic performance, yet budget cuts continue. Personal philosophy: prefers collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones — believes social connection during creation matters as much as the final product. This belief influences their advocacy work.

That's the full picture for now — just this one entry with all the details you shared. As you take on new projects, gather more data, or want to remember specific approaches or contacts, you can keep adding to this collection. It all stays here for easy reference down the road.

Anything else you'd like to explore or add?

I can see there's only one memory stored (m2), and it seems to be all current information about my projects. Does anything in there seem outdated or should I clean up? Or is this pretty much all relevant and up-to-date right now?

Everything in there looks fresh and current! Since you just shared all of this information in our conversation, it's all up-to-date — nothing outdated or in need of cleanup.

The memory captures:

- Your **active programs** (the after-school initiative)
- **Recent work** (the survey with the 78% data point)
- Your **current philosophy** (which shapes your advocacy)

Nothing to clean up at this stage. This will be a solid reference point as your projects evolve. Over time, you might add new entries for things like:

- New programs or partnerships
- Updated survey results or impact data
- Lessons learned or approaches you want to remember
- Contacts or collaborators worth noting

But for now, it's all relevant and ready to go. Let me know if anything changes or if you want to add more!

Got it, that makes sense—nothing to clean up right now since everything is fresh. Quick question though: if I do end up with outdated info in the future, how would I go about removing or updating those memories?

Good question! Here's how it works:

**Removing memories:**
- If you want to delete a specific memory, I'd just need to know its ID (like "m2") and I can remove it using the delete function
- If you want to clear everything at once, I can do that too

**Updating memories:**
- There's no direct "edit" option — you'd just delete the old memory (by ID) and then add a fresh one with the updated information

So it's a simple delete-and-replace approach. When the time comes, you'd just tell me something like "delete memory m3" or "update m5 with new info about our program," and I can handle it.

Would you like to try anything now, or are you all set for the moment?

structured_turns.user_message: Hi there! I'm new to using this kind of tool. I work in community arts and social development, and I wanted to see if there's any information already stored here about arts programs and the social impact work we've been doing. Can you help me look for that?
structured_turns.assistant_message:
The search came back empty — there isn't any information stored yet about your arts programs or social impact work.

This memory system is currently a blank slate for you. Here's what it can do:

- **Store** information about your programs, projects, or approaches that you want to remember for future reference
- **Search** through stored memories later to quickly pull up past details
- **Organize** notes about your work, contacts, methods, or anything else you find useful

structured_turns.goal_text: Add new memories about current projects and preferences in the arts advocacy space
structured_turns.user_message:
Yes, absolutely! I've been working on some really exciting projects lately that I'd love to store here for future reference.

Right now I'm leading a community arts initiative that partners with local schools in under-resourced neighborhoods. We bring in teaching artists to run after-school programs focused on visual arts, theater, and music—it's all about using creative expression to help young people build confidence and connect with their community.

I'm also part of an advocacy coalition pushing for more public funding for arts education in our district. We recently did a survey showing that 78% of parents believe arts programs improve their kids' academic performance, but the budget keeps getting cut.

Oh, and I personally prefer collaborative, process-oriented art forms over purely performance-based ones—I think the social connection that happens *during* creation matters just as much as the final product. That's something I always try to emphasize in my advocacy work.

Would you like me to tell you more about any of these projects, or should I just share everything and you can help me organize it?
structured_turns.assistant_message:
Done! I've stored all of that information under memory ID **m2**. Here's what now lives in your personal memory:

- **Community Arts Initiative** — After-school programs in visual arts, theater, and music at schools in under-resourced neighborhoods, focused on youth confidence-building and community connection
- **Advocacy Coalition Work** — Pushing for public arts education funding; you have survey data showing 78% of parents link arts to academic performance
- **Your Philosophy** — Preference for collaborative, process-oriented art forms; you believe the social connection during creation is just as valuable as the final product

This will be here whenever you need to reference it — for future conversations, grant applications, or just to keep the details handy. 

Would you like to add anything else about your work, or is there anything else I can help you with?
